title: Reading and Use of English – Part 3
description: For questions 17–24, read the text below. Use the word given in CAPITALS at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.

content.text: Understanding what drives us to achieve our goals is (0) ESSENTIAL (ESSENCE) for personal growth. Psychologists distinguish between intrinsic and extrinsic motivation, both of which are (17) SIGNIFICANT (SIGNIFY) in different contexts. While rewards can be (18) EFFECTIVE (EFFECT) in the short term, long-term (19) COMMITMENT (COMMIT) usually comes from within. Many individuals find that setting (20) REALISTIC (REAL) targets helps maintain their focus and prevents (21) FRUSTRATION (FRUSTRATE). Furthermore, the (22) RECOGNITION (RECOGNIZE) of small achievements can boost self-esteem and (23) STRENGTHEN (STRONG) one's resolve. However, maintaining a high level of (24) ENTHUSIASM (ENTHUSIAST) can be challenging when faced with setbacks. To overcome these, one must develop a resilient mindset and learn to view failures as (25) OPPORTUNITIES (OPPORTUNE) for learning. By cultivating a positive environment and surrounding oneself with supportive people, it becomes easier to stay motivated and continue striving for excellence in all areas of life, from professional careers to personal hobbies and relationships.

example.text: Understanding what drives us... is (0) ESSENTIAL (ESSENCE) for personal growth.
example.explanation: Adjective required after the verb 'is'; ESSENCE → ESSENTIAL.

questions.explanation: Adjective required to describe 'motivation'; SIGNIFY → SIGNIFICANT.
Adjective required to modify 'rewards'; EFFECT → EFFECTIVE.
Noun required as the subject of the clause; COMMIT → COMMITMENT.
Adjective required to modify 'targets'; REAL → REALISTIC.
Noun required after the definite article; RECOGNIZE → RECOGNITION.
